title: elaeodendron dioicum var. acuminatum
intro: Elaeodendron dioicum var. acuminatum, also known as the prickly ash tree, is a plant species that belongs to the family Celastraceae. The prickly ash tree is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating. The bark and roots of the plant have been traditionally used in herbal preparations to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of the prickly ash tree can be consumed as a tea, tincture, or powder to reap its health benefits, including improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function.
